description: Figure 7 displays X-ray diffraction patterns of Fe–30Mn–10Al–1.5C–xBe (x = 0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5) strips after rolling at 1050 °C, revealing the effect of Be addition on phase constitution. The baseline 0Be and 0.5Be alloys show only austenite (γ) diffraction peaks indexed as (111), (200), (220), (311), and (222), indicating a single-phase austenitic matrix under these conditions. At 1.0 wt% Be, new peaks assigned to δ-ferrite [(110), (200), (211)] appear alongside intermetallic reflections from Be11Fe [(112), (303)] and Be2Fe (212). Further increasing Be to 1.5 wt% intensifies the δ-ferrite and Be-Fe compound peaks while retaining the γ-austenite matrix. The progressive emergence of these phases confirms that Be solubility in austenite is limited; once saturated, excess Be segregates to form δ-ferrite surrounding austenite and nucleates Be11Fe and Be2Fe precipitates within the grains, supporting the proposed solidification and phase partitioning mechanism.
